mnemonics: HASH - Hashed, Append-Only, Secure, Holds Consensus
keyPoints: A blockchain is a distributed, immutable ledger.
Consensus mechanisms (e.g., Proof-of-Work, Proof-of-Stake) validate transactions.
Smart contracts are self-executing code stored on the blockchain.
commonMistakes: Assuming all data on a blockchain is private; most data is public and transparent.
Confusing a distributed ledger with a decentralized database; they have different security and trust models.
Equating blockchain solely with cryptocurrency; it has many other applications like supply chain tracking.
